audit_status.source_base: Greek Galatians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Spirit received by trust, Abraham, curse/law, promise before Law, paidagogos, baptism, one in Messiah.

verses.restored: O foolish Galatians, who bewitched you, before whose eyes Jesus Messiah was publicly portrayed as crucified?
This only I want to learn from you: did you receive the Spirit from works of Law or from hearing of trust?
Are you so foolish? Having begun by Spirit, are you now being completed by flesh?
Did you suffer so many things in vain, if indeed also in vain?
Therefore the One supplying the Spirit to you and working powers among you, is it from works of Law or from hearing of trust?
Just as Abraham trusted the Divine, and it was counted to him as righteousness/justice.
Know then that those from trust, these are children of Abraham.
And the Scripture, foreseeing that the Divine makes the nations righteous/just from trust, announced good news beforehand to Abraham: In you all the nations will be blessed.
So then those from trust are blessed with trusting Abraham.
For as many as are from works of Law are under curse, for it is written: Cursed is everyone who does not remain in all things written in the book of the Law to do them.
Now that no one is made righteous/just before the Divine by Law is clear, because: The righteous/just one will live from trust.
But the Law is not from trust; rather: The one doing these things will live by them.
Messiah redeemed/released us from the curse of the Law, having become a curse for us, for it is written: Cursed is everyone hanging upon a tree,
so that the blessing of Abraham might come into the nations in Messiah Jesus, so that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through trust.
Siblings, I speak according to human terms: even a human covenant having been confirmed, no one sets aside or adds to it.
Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. It does not say, and to seeds, as upon many, but as upon one: and to your seed, who is Messiah.
This I say: the Law, having come four hundred thirty years later, does not invalidate a covenant previously confirmed by the Divine, so as to make the promise inactive.
For if the inheritance is from Law, it is no longer from promise; but to Abraham the Divine has graciously given it through promise.
Why then the Law? It was added because of transgressions until the seed should come to whom the promise had been made, arranged through messengers in the hand of a mediator.
Now a mediator is not of one, but the Divine is one.
Is the Law then against the promises of the Divine? May it not be. For if a Law had been given able to make alive, righteousness/justice truly would have been from Law.
But the Scripture enclosed all things under sin/misalignment, so that the promise from trust/faithfulness of Jesus Messiah might be given to those trusting.
But before trust came, we were guarded under Law, being enclosed until the coming trust would be unveiled.
So the Law has become our paidagogos/guardian until Messiah, so that we might be made righteous/just from trust.
But trust having come, we are no longer under a paidagogos/guardian.
For you are all sons/children of the Divine through trust in Messiah Jesus.
For as many of you as were baptized into Messiah have clothed yourselves with Messiah.
There is no Judean nor Greek, there is no enslaved nor free, there is no male and female; for you all are one in Messiah Jesus.
And if you are Messiah's, then you are Abraham's seed, heirs according to promise.

translation_notes: Law is not against the promises; Paul says the Law could not make alive, while promise/Spirit/trust define nations-inclusion.
Paidagogos is kept with guardian to avoid reducing the image to modern schoolteacher.
Galatians 3:28 is baptismal unity language with real social implications, not erasure of embodied difference.
